violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Case of food on floor in walk in freezer.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Food removed from original packaging was not placed in a container with an impervious and nonabsorbent cover. -BBQ sauce and seasonings at end of cook line were not covered between uses.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Soiled, over-filled cloths in sani bucket at end of cook line.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Stained cambros in clean storage.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Frost build up at interior of icecream bin on cook line.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. -Stainless scrubber left in hand sink next to dish pit.--No splash guard around hand sink at clean side of dishwasher drain board and no splash guard for clean dishes storage rack on other side of the same handsink.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. -Bar hand sink hand soap was not at hand sink (located at far end of bar).

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Grease/debris at line/wall of broiler. -Odor at far end of cook line. -Ice build up at back wall and floor of walk in freezer.

Use of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not used and/or applied according to manufacturer's label. -Sanitizer dispenser is making solutions over 400ppm Quat. *Service tech has been called, buckets will need to be manually diluted to 200-400ppm Quat until unit is corrected.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Several cases of food stored on floor in walk in freezer.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Wet cloth left at hand sink. -Damp cloth on prep surface at right make table. -Wet cloth left out of bucket at salad make table. -Sani bucket at bar found with less than 150ppm Quat.

Preventing contamination by consumers. — --Utensil was not suitable or dispensed effectively for self-service operations of ready-to-eat foods. -Single use paper bowl left in/used as a scoop in pico at make line. -Single use portion cup used as scoop in hot hold cheese at make line. -Single use portion cup left in/used as scoop in dressing at expo line.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Right side make line found with temperatures of 45F at top and bottom of unit. -Upright stainless refrigerator at end of cookline found with food temperatures of 45F. *Directed employees to ice or move products to return temperatures to 41F or less and to have units serviced ASAP.

Hot storage of potentially hazardous food. — --Hot food storage facilities/equipment was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining temperature of potentially hazardous food at 140F or greater. -Hot hold at make line found with temperatures of 125F. *Water was low in unit and refilled while on-site. *Directed staff to dispose of items at 3pm since they had been placed at approximately 11 a.m.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Sheet pans for use at fryers were cut in half and welded at corners.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. -Rough corners/bottoms and door interiors of prep tables at cook line.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. *Surfaces throughout facility are in need of thorough cleaning: -Residue at underside of make line handles. -Grease/debris at tops/sides of cookline equipment. -Residue scale and prep table at back prep area. -Water spot/limescale at steamer surfaces. -Residue on outside surfaces/handles of bins stored below back stainless prep table. -Residue on exteriors of spice storage containers. -Ice scoop holders at server line had floaties/debris at bottom interiors. -Sticky residue/cup stuck on top of beer cooler. -Heavy residue at bottom surface and liner at interior of bar refrigerator.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Bar Sani sink due for refresh-made at ~10:30 a.m. found at ~150ppm Quat and cloudy at 3:20 p.m.

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. -Facility does not have Quat test strips. *Facility has test strips for the sink and surface sanitizer which is no longer in use, it was replaced with Oasis Multi-Quat sanitizer.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. -Bar hand sink is blocked by strainer and is being used as dump sink. *Staff stated dump sink has a leak and is currently disconnected.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc. …) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Residue/debris at back floor corner in walk in cooler. -Dust debris a walk in cooler fans.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc. …) were not maintained in good repair. -Floor seal and tiles chipped at cookline and missing/cracked tiles in walk in cooler. -Missing ceiling tile near ice machine.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. -Dirty aprons stored hanging on clean dishes storage rack. -Personal drink stored on clean dishes rack. -Cup of water without lid stored on roll of paper towels at back prep area. -drinks
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (2 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Thermometers provided, used, clean, and accurate (1 pt deducted)
